(born August 28, 1984) is an American professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") who formerly competed in the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")'s [Flyweight](/wiki/Flyweight_(MMA) "Flyweight (MMA)") division. A professional competitor since 2009, Gaudinot has also formerly competed on [Spike TV](/wiki/Spike_TV "Spike TV")'s [The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Bisping_vs._Team_Miller "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller") as a [Bantamweight](/wiki/Bantamweight_(MMA) "Bantamweight (MMA)").

**Louis J. Gaudinot** (born August 28, 1984) is an American professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") who formerly competed in the [UFC](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")'s [Flyweight](/wiki/Flyweight_(MMA) "Flyweight (MMA)") division. A professional competitor since 2009, Gaudinot has also formerly competed on [Spike TV](/wiki/Spike_TV "Spike TV")'s [The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Bisping_vs._Team_Miller "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller") as a [Bantamweight](/wiki/Bantamweight_(MMA) "Bantamweight (MMA)").

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Gaudinot began training in [martial arts](/wiki/Martial_arts "Martial arts") with [Tiger Schulmann](/wiki/Tiger_Schulmann "Tiger Schulmann") well over twenty years ago[*[as of?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*] because of where he grew up and how small he was as a child. Being tiny in a disinvested neighborhood, Louis's parents knew he would have to defend himself at one or many points in his life, so they brought him to the Tiger Schulmann's in Yonkers NY when he was six years old.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Originally, he trained in [Kyokushin Karate](/wiki/Kyokushin_Karate "Kyokushin Karate") and over time began also training in [grappling](/wiki/Grappling "Grappling").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Gaudinot later earned a fourth-degree black belt in [karate](/wiki/Karate "Karate").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

After many years of training, in 2008, Gaudinot made his amateur debut. He held a 3–1 record as an amateur before turning professional in 2009.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Ring of Combat

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ring of Combat")]

Gaudinot signed with New Jersey's Ring of Combat to compete as a professional in April 2009. His debut came at Ring of Combat 24 against fellow 0-0 fighter, Chris Aquino. Gaudinot controlled the bout and won the fight via unanimous decision after two, four-minute rounds. After the fight, he stirred some controversy when he flipped off Aquino's corner.

Gaudinot fought on the next card at Ring of Combat 25 and took his first professional loss, losing a unanimous decision to Nick Cottone.

At Ring of Combat 26, Gaudinot bounced back from his loss to Cottone with a second-round [TKO](/wiki/Technical_knockout "Technical knockout") victory over Jeff Cressman. Two months later Gaudinot was again in action and again was fighting on back-to-back Ring of Combat cards. On the Ring of Combat 27 card he competed in his first three-round fight, winning via unanimous decision against Nate Williams.

Gaudinot fought against Tuan Pham at Ring of Combat 28, winning the fight via [KO](/wiki/KO "KO") in the first round. The impressive victory earned Gaudinot a chance at the vacant Ring of Combat Flyweight Championship. His title shot came at Ring of Combat 31 against Jesse Riggleman. Gaudinot dominated the bout and won via submission ([guillotine choke](/wiki/Guillotine_choke "Guillotine choke")) near the end of the first round.

### The Ultimate Fighter

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: The Ultimate Fighter")]

In 2011, Gaudinot signed with the UFC to compete on [The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Bisping_vs._Team_Miller "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller").[[7]](#cite_note-7) In the first episode, Gaudinot faced [Paul McVeigh](/wiki/Paul_McVeigh_(fighter) "Paul McVeigh (fighter)"); winning via TKO in the third round.[[8]](#cite_note-8) The win gained Gaudinot entry into the *Ultimate Fighter* house. He was selected as a part of Team Bisping.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Gaudinot next fought against Team Mayhem's [Dustin Pague](/wiki/Dustin_Pague "Dustin Pague"). Gaudinot lost the fight in the second round via [rear-naked choke](/wiki/Rear-naked_choke "Rear-naked choke") submission.[[10]](#cite_note-Episode_4-10) The fight also won Gaudinot and Pague an additional $25,000 for the fan-voted "Fight of the Season".

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Gaudinot made his official UFC debut on December 3, 2011, at [The Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_14_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale") against [Johnny Bedford](/wiki/Johnny_Bedford "Johnny Bedford"). Gaudinot lost to Bedford via TKO in the third round.

Gaudinot returned to the [flyweight](/wiki/Flyweight_(MMA) "Flyweight (MMA)") division and faced former [Jungle Fight Bantamweight Champion](/wiki/Jungle_Fight "Jungle Fight"), [John Lineker](/wiki/John_Lineker "John Lineker"), on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3").[[11]](#cite_note-11) The bout was contested at a catchweight of 127 lb, as Lineker missed weight. Gaudinot defeated Lineker via [guillotine choke](/wiki/Guillotine_choke "Guillotine choke") submission in the second round after an action-packed, back-and-forth first two rounds. Both participants earned *Fight of the Night* honors for their performances.

Gaudinot was expected to face [Darren Uyenoyama](/wiki/Darren_Uyenoyama "Darren Uyenoyama") on October 5, 2012, at [UFC on FX 5](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_5 "UFC on FX 5").[[12]](#cite_note-12) However, Gaudinot was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by promotional newcomer [Phil Harris](/wiki/Phil_Harris_(fighter) "Phil Harris (fighter)").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Gaudinot faced [Tim Elliott](/wiki/Tim_Elliott "Tim Elliott") on August 31, 2013, at [UFC 164](/wiki/UFC_164 "UFC 164").[[14]](#cite_note-14) He lost the fight via dominant unanimous decision.

Gaudinot faced [Phil Harris](/wiki/Phil_Harris_(fighter) "Phil Harris (fighter)") at [UFC Fight Night 37](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_37 "UFC Fight Night 37").[[15]](#cite_note-15) He won the fight via submission in the first round. However, on June 20, the win was overturned to a No Contest after it was revealed that Gaudinot had failed his drug test.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Gaudinot was expected to face [Patrick Holohan](/wiki/Patrick_Holohan "Patrick Holohan") on October 4, 2014, at [UFC Fight Night: MacDonald vs. Saffiedine](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night:_MacDonald_vs._Saffiedine "UFC Fight Night: MacDonald vs. Saffiedine"),[[17]](#cite_note-17) but pulled out of the bout due to injury.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Gaudinot faced [Kyoji Horiguchi](/wiki/Kyoji_Horiguchi "Kyoji Horiguchi") on January 3, 2015, at [UFC 182](/wiki/UFC_182 "UFC 182").[[19]](#cite_note-19) He lost the fight by unanimous decision and was subsequently released from the organization.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### Post-UFC career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Post-UFC career")]

In his first fight since being released from the UFC, Gaudinot faced off against [Bellator](/wiki/Bellator_MMA "Bellator MMA") veteran [Claudio Ledesma](/wiki/Claudio_Ledesma "Claudio Ledesma") in the main event of CFFC 51 on September 12, 2015.[[21]](#cite_note-21) Gaudinot made a successful rebound from a three-fight slump, winning the fight with a dominant unanimous decision verdict.

The win over Ledesma granted Gaudinot a title shot against the CFFC Flyweight Champion Sidemar Honório, which took place at CFFC 57 on March 19, 2016. He won the fight via first-round knockout, claiming the championship which he later vacated with no title defenses.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

Gaudinot then challenged Phil Caracappa for the Ring of Combat Bantamweight Championship at ROC 65 on September 21, 2018.[[23]](#cite_note-23) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

## Bare-knuckle boxing

### Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship")]

Gaudinot was expected to make his debut against Sean Santella in a flyweight bout at [BKFC 61](/wiki/2024_in_Bare_Knuckle_Fighting_Championship#BKFC_61_Connecticut:_Rivera_vs._Straus "2024 in Bare Knuckle Fighting Championship") on May 11, 2024.[[25]](#cite_note-25) However, on April 24, 2024, the bout was pulled from the card for unknown reasons.

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

### Mixed martial arts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Gaudinot&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts")]

* **[Cage Fury Fighting Championships](/wiki/Cage_Fury_Fighting_Championships "Cage Fury Fighting Championships")**
  + [CFFC Flyweight Champion](/wiki/Cage_Fury_Fighting_Championships#CFFC_Flyweight_Championship "Cage Fury Fighting Championships") (One time; former)
* **Ring of Combat**
  + Ring of Combat Flyweight Champion (One time)
* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + Fight of the Season ([The Ultimate Fighter 14](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_14 "The Ultimate Fighter 14"))
  + [Fight Of The Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") (One time)
  + **UFC.com Awards**
    - 2012: [Ranked #10 Fight of the Year](/wiki/2012_in_UFC#2012_UFC.com_awards "2012 in UFC") vs. [John Lineker](/wiki/John_Lineker "John Lineker")[[26]](#cite_note-26)

| Win | 1–0 | Paul McVeigh | TKO (elbow and punches) | [The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Bisping_vs._Team_Miller "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller") | 2011 | 3 | 4:59 | [Las Vegas, Nevada](/wiki/Las_Vegas,_Nevada "Las Vegas, Nevada"), United States | Preliminary Bout. |
